name: Mediterranean Lamb Burgers
recipeIngredient: 1 garlic clove, minced into a paste
3 tablespoons shredded fresh mint leaves, or to taste
1 1.5-pound package of fresh ground lamb from the shoulder
1 tablespoon minced fresh rosemary, or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary crumbled
1 tablespoon minced fresh parsley leaves, or 1 teaspoon dried thyme
salt and freshly ground black pepper
1/3 cup coarsely chopped pitted kalamata olives
2 ripe medium tomatoes, seeded and finely chopped
6 pita bread, each split halfway around the edge to form a pocket
recipeInstructions:
1. Make yogurt sauce: Drain the yogurt in a sieve lined with a dampened paper towel set over a bowl for 30 minutes. Transfer the drained yogurt to a small bowl and stir in the garlic paste and mint.
2. Make burgers: Prepare the grill by lightly oiling the rack. In a bowl, gently but thoroughly combine the lamb, minced garlic, rosemary (fresh or dried), parsley (fresh or dried), salt, and black pepper. Grill on an oiled rack set 5 to 6 inches over glowing coals for 7 minutes on each side for medium-rare.
3. In a bowl, combine the olives, chopped tomatoes, parsley, olive oil, red wine vinegar, and season with salt and pepper.
4. Place burgers in pita pockets and sprinkle with goat cheese. Top with the chopped salad. Serve burgers with yogurt sauce.
5. Yield: 4 servings
